2015-03-01.title: Mexico Moves to Save Endangered Porpoise

2015-03-01.title: Germans at the Crux of Crises, but Reluctantly

2015-03-01.title: Tupperware’s Sweet Spot Shifts to Indonesia
2015-03-01.abstract: As in 1960s America, many Indonesian women stay at home, creating an audience for parties run by women who have begun to sidle past conservative mores and into the work force.

2015-03-01.title: South Korean President Urges Japan to Admit Past Wrongs

2015-03-01.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: Americans respond to the vessel’s seizure in 1915.

2015-03-02.abstract: Nineteen of the Assyrian Christians seized by Islamic State militants last week in northeastern Syria have been freed, and they met with church officials in a cathedral in the city of Hasaka, local leaders said Sunday.

2015-03-02.title: Shoestring Legal Aid Group Helps Poor in Rural India
2015-03-02.abstract: A team of young lawyers have abandoned other careers and helped found the group to aid people in Chhattisgarh, where human rights abuses are often overlooked.

2015-03-02.title: Kerry Is Pushing for Agreement in Iran Nuclear Talks
2015-03-02.abstract: With so much at stake in the Iranian nuclear talks, Secretary of State John Kerry’s relentless negotiating style and repeated meetings with Iran’s foreign minister have become part of the debate.

2015-03-03.abstract: Admission to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s speech to Congress on Tuesday was “hotter than fresh latkes,” Senator Charles E. Schumer of New York said.

2015-03-03.abstract: Dr. Zakir Naik, a prominent Muslim from India, has said that “the Jews” control America and that the United States government was responsible for the Sept. 11 attacks.

2015-03-03.abstract: The generals, including the son of a former high-ranking leader, were the latest to fall under President Xi Jinping’s sweeping anticorruption campaign.

2015-03-04.abstract: Namibia’s departing president, Hifikepunye Pohamba, has been awarded the 2014 Ibrahim Prize for Achievement in African Leadership.

2015-03-04.abstract: Some economists have called Ethiopia an “African Lion,” and the government has an ambitious plan to make Ethiopia a middle-income country by 2025.

2015-03-04.abstract: The younger generation took offense, feeling that the song characterized them as lazy and unwilling to make an effort in life by adults who seemed dismissive of the obstacles they face.

2015-03-06.abstract: Citing a report by investigators that she had hired, Sandra Arroyo Salgado, a judge and the former wife of Mr. Nisman, said, “Suicide and an accident are totally ruled out.”

2015-03-06.abstract: A leadership body called for curtailing the longstanding cooperation with Israel, but left final decisions to President Mahmoud Abbas and the Palestine Liberation Organization’s executive committee.

2015-03-08.abstract: Hundreds of African, Western and American troops joined forces in military training exercises aimed at warding off the expansion of terrorist groups.

2015-03-08.abstract: The captain and co-pilot of Flight 370, which disappeared a year ago, had shown no signs of distress, a Malaysian government report said Sunday.

2015-03-25.abstract: This video from the French Interior Ministry shows the scene of the Germanwings Airbus A320 crash in the French Alps.

2015-03-25.title: Rebukes From White House Risk Buoying Netanyahu
2015-03-25.abstract: Tensions between Israel and the U.S. have soared, and even Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics have been astonished by President Obama’s criticism of the Israeli leader.

2015-03-25.title: In Nuclear Talks, Iran Seeks to Avoid Specifics
2015-03-25.abstract: If an agreement to limit Iran’s nuclear capability is reached by the deadline in the next seven days, one thing may be missing: an actual written accord, signed by the Iranians.

2015-03-25.title: Same-Sex Benefits Survive a Vote
2015-03-25.abstract: The United States joined the effort to defeat a Russian proposal that would have invalidated the benefits expansion to United Nations employees who are spouses in same-sex marriages.

2015-03-26.title: Victims of Germanwings Flight 9525
2015-03-26.abstract: Passengers from at least 15 countries were aboard the flight from Barcelona, Spain, to Düsseldorf, Germany, including 72 Germans and 35 Spaniards. Here are profiles of some of the 123 victims whose families have been notified.

2015-03-26.abstract: Israel’s president also chided Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for warning that Arab citizens were flocking to polling places in “droves.”

2015-03-26.title: Cost a Factor in Streaming Flight Data in Real Time
2015-03-26.abstract: Another plane crash has many in aviation asking whether commercial flights should be required to transmit live data in real time.

2015-03-26.abstract: A 63-page report by the human rights organization came a week before a formal Palestinian accession to the International Criminal Court in The Hague.

2015-03-26.title: The Crisis in Yemen: What You Need to Know
2015-03-26.abstract: Yemen, the poorest country in the Middle East, is embroiled in a struggle for power that has serious implications for the region and the security of the United States.

2015-03-27.abstract: If Andreas Lubitz, the flight’s 27-year-old co-pilot, was determined to kill himself, he would not be the first to use an airplane as a tool for self-destruction or to kill passengers and other crew members in the process.

2015-03-27.abstract: Twitter and Facebook updates and snatches of grainy video on Instagram and YouTube offered observers following from afar vivid glimpses of the terror.

2015-03-27.abstract: By apparently locking the captain out of the cockpit, the co-pilot of Germanwings Flight 9525 took advantage of a major safety protocol instituted after the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.

2015-03-27.abstract: The authors have written a rollicking account of the acquisition of Chinese art and antiquities by Americans who came to China in the 19th and 20th centuries.

2015-03-27.abstract: In becoming involved in a Saudi-led campaign to dislodge rebels in Yemen, the United States has created more entanglement with its alliances.

2015-03-28.abstract: The Shabab claimed responsibility for the attack, in which at least seven people were killed, including a Somali ambassador.

2015-03-28.abstract: Burning a 75-foot-tall pagan temple in a Republican Catholic enclave on the loyalist Protestant side of Londonderry seemed to many like a bad idea.

2015-03-28.abstract: Eric Volz turned a wrongful conviction in Nicaragua into a vocation, helping other Americans imprisoned overseas.

2015-03-28.abstract: The provision of ayahuasca, a psychedelic brew used in the Amazon basin for centuries, to inmates on short furloughs reflects a quest to ease pressure on Brazil’s prison system.

2015-03-28.abstract: In a 10-minute address broadcast on Yemeni television, Ali Abdullah Saleh called for elections to choose a new president.

2015-03-29.abstract: The participants from Dnepropetrovsk had hoped to help mend a rift between President Petro O. Poroshenko and the regional governor he fired.

2015-03-29.abstract: The decision to join the bank is an about-face for the nation, which had earlier agreed with the Obama administration’s requests to stay aloof from the bank.

2015-03-29.abstract: An Airbus A320 landed among a group of antennas, shearing off its landing gear, but no one was seriously injured, officials said.

2015-03-30.abstract: Farkhunda has emerged as the rarest of Afghan figures: a female religious champion who ventured into a shrine to preach Islam and was felled by ignorant men.

2015-03-31.abstract: Egyptian prosecutors are bringing criminal charges against witnesses who said they saw the police kill an unarmed poet and activist during a demonstration, a lawyer who has seen the charges said on Monday.

2015-03-31.abstract: President Obama will travel to Kenya in July to co-host a forum on entrepreneurship as part of an effort to support economic development in Africa.

2015-03-31.abstract: The intense focus on the co-pilot’s mental illness has raised concerns that it could stigmatize mentally ill people and make it harder to identify troubled workers.

2015-03-31.abstract: Government forces were routed from Idlib, a provincial capital in the country’s north.
